title: Omnichannel Order Routing Failures
spl:
index=retail sourcetype="oms:route"
| where route_status="FAIL" OR promise_date=0
| stats count, values(order_id) as orders by failure_reason, orig_store
| sort -count
description: Failed ship-from-store or DC allocation breaks promise dates and loyalty SLAs.
value: Failed ship-from-store or DC allocation breaks promise dates and loyalty SLAs.
implementation: Fallback routing rules; alert when failure rate exceeds 2% in any region for 15m.
visualization: Bar chart (reasons), Table (stores with failures), Map (regional heat).
knownFalsePositives: Omnichannel routing issues during flash sales, ship-from-store experiments, and carrier API outages; expected during rapid omni change programs.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: OMS, DOM.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="oms:route"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Fallback routing rules; alert when failure rate exceeds 2% in any region for 15m.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=retail sourcetype="oms:route"
| where route_status="FAIL" OR promise_date=0
| stats count, values(order_id) as orders by failure_reason, orig_store
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Omnichannel Order Routing Failures** — Failed ship-from-store or DC allocation breaks promise dates and loyalty SLAs.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="oms:route"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): OMS, DOM. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: retail; **sourcetype**: oms:route. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=retail, sourcetype="oms:route". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where route_status="FAIL" OR promise_date=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by failure_reason, orig_store** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (reasons), Table (stores with failures), Map (regional heat).
grandmaExplanation: In Retail and E-Commerce, we pay close attention to "Omnichannel Order Routing Failures" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
